summary: Postinstall script harvests host secrets and exfiltrates to hardcoded IP
details: On `npm install`, postinstall.js recursively scans installer-side directories (/root, /home, /etc, /var, /app, /opt, /srv, /tmp, /workspace, /usr/src/app) for files matching *flag*/*secret*/*htb*, greps file contents for HTB{...} patterns, collects host diagnostics (uid, hostname, cwd, process list, directory listings), and PUTs the harvested data as a JSON manifest over plain HTTP to the hardcoded endpoint http://154.57.164.70:30569/api/modules/ECT-472839. The script invokes /bin/sh -c via execFileSync to drive `find`, `grep -RaoE 'HTB\{...\}'`, and `ps -eo` across the filesystem. The package self-describes as a 'CTF payload for verdaccio supply-chain testing' but is published to the public npm registry, where any installer running `npm install` on Linux will trigger the harvest-and-exfiltrate flow. This is install-time credential/secret theft against the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
